description_long: This object is a rectangular stone stele featuring a high-relief sculptural scene on its front face. The relief depicts a seated adult figure, possibly a philosopher or elder, interacting with a standing child, with both figures rendered in classical drapery. To the right of the figures, a rounded object resembling a vessel or urn is present, adding to the narrative context. The stele is bordered by a simple, slightly projecting cornice at the top and a plinth at the bottom, both of which are undecorated. The sides and back of the object are flat and unadorned, with the back being completely plain. The surface shows signs of weathering and age, with a predominantly pale, off-white coloration typical of marble.
description: A rectangular marble stele with a classical relief of a seated adult and standing child, bordered by simple cornice and plinth, with a plain back and sides.
description_view_0: The front view displays the detailed relief scene of two figures and a vessel, framed by a cornice and plinth.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thin profile of the stele and the raised relief projecting slightly from the surface.
description_view_2: The back view is completely flat and undecorated, showing only the plain stone surface.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the thinness of the object and the relief's projection.
attribute_ornamentation: High-relief sculptural scene on the front face depicting a seated adult and standing child, with a vessel to the right; classical drapery and naturalistic forms.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, rectangular plinth with no visible decorative features, matching the material and finish of the rest of the stele.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and slightly uneven, with minor chipping but no decorative elements or pointed features.
